chunk_summary: This section discusses carbohydrate intolerance syndromes in children, focusing on dietary management within the broader context of macronutrient nutrition during childhood.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of carbohydrates in childhood nutrition and their potential intolerance syndromes.
Identify dietary strategies to manage carbohydrate intolerance in children.
Recognize the symptoms and diagnostic approaches for carbohydrate intolerance.
Explore the impact of carbohydrate intolerance on overall childhood growth and development.
key_facts: Carbohydrate intolerance in children can result from enzyme deficiencies such as lactase or sucrase-isomaltase deficiency.
Symptoms of carbohydrate intolerance include bloating, diarrhea, and abdominal pain after carbohydrate ingestion.
Dietary management often involves reducing or eliminating specific carbohydrates, such as lactose or sucrose, from the diet.
Lactose intolerance affects approximately 65% of the global population, with varying prevalence among ethnic groups.
Early diagnosis and dietary adjustments are critical to prevent growth and developmental delays in children with carbohydrate intolerance.
reference_quote: Carbohydrate intolerance syndromes in children require prompt dietary intervention to prevent long-term nutritional deficiencies and growth impairments.
